title: bridges: std::sync::Mutex .lock().unwrap() panics if lock poisoned
description: bridge-jmap/src/bridge.rs, bridge-slack/src/bridge.rs: `Arc<std::sync::Mutex<_>>` locks are acquired with `.lock().unwrap()`. If any async task panics while holding a lock, the mutex is poisoned and all subsequent `.lock().unwrap()` calls panic, crashing the bridge daemon. The critical sections are currently safe (HashSet ops), but the pattern is fragile. Fix: replace `std::sync::Mutex` with `parking_lot::Mutex` (which never poisons) or use `.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` to recover from poisoning gracefully.
